title: round4_phase1_complete
analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 exhaustive counterparty analysis on Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 days 1–3 (4281 trades merged to 4281 event rows). Forward moves = K steps along price tape for same (day, product). Aggression = price vs concurrent BBO (aggr_buy / aggr_sell / passive_mid). Script: manual_traders/R4/r4_counterparty_gated/run_r4_phase1_analysis.py

phase1_extras_against_prompt.per_trade_event_file: r4_phase1_trade_events.csv — all 4281 events with K∈{5,20,100} fwd_mids, fwd_ext, ts_bucket, spread_bucket, burst_ge4, aggression (stratify in pandas without re-joining).
phase1_extras_against_prompt.median_and_bootstrap_95: r4_participant_fwd_by_symbol.csv now includes median_fwd and boot_ci95_mean_lo/hi (2000 resamples) per participant×role×symbol×K; n≥20 for bootstrap. Example Mark67 extract K=5: mean 1.96, CI [1.67,2.25], median 2.0.
phase1_extras_against_prompt.day_stability: r4_participant_fwd_by_symbol_by_day.csv: same cells with n≥5 in a single day (compare across days 1–3).
phase1_extras_against_prompt.stratified_session_spread_burst: r4_stratified_markout_by_session_spread_burst.csv (n≥10 per cell): (participant, U_aggr_buy/sell, product)×K×ts_bucket×spread_bucket×burst_ge4; mean, median, t, bootstrap CI, frac_pos. 258 cells; run_r4_phase1_stratify_markouts.py. Mark 67 extract aggr buy K=5 stays positive in tight-spread + non-burst + every ts_bucket with enough n. r4_stratified_markout_cells_n20_t2.csv = |t|≥2, n≥20 for screening.
phase1_extras_against_prompt.graph_reciprocity_hubs: r4_edge_pairs_with_reciprocity.csv (reverse pair counts, reciprocity ratio), r4_node_degree_in_out_top30.csv, r4_top20_directed_edge_pairs.csv. gross_notional = sum |price*qty| per directed pair.
phase1_extras_against_prompt.cluster_volume_balance: r4_participant_net_flow_r4.csv: per-name buy vs sell row counts and sum(|p*q|) — Mark 14 near symmetric MM; Mark 67 buy-only in tape rows (0 sell-side).
phase1_extras_against_prompt.burst_orchestrator: r4_burst_orchestrator_by_timestamp.csv: mode buyer/seller on each (day,timestamp) with ≥2 prints; Mark01/Mark22 dominate most multi-print ticks on this slice.
phase1_extras_against_prompt.residual_positive_tail: r4_baseline_resid_pos_tail_n20.csv: top mean residual (buyer,seller,symbol) with n_cell≥20 after global pair×symbol mean baseline (supplements trade-level r4_trade_level_residual_fwd20.csv).
phase1_extras_against_prompt.passive_lp_markouts: r4_passive_seller_after_aggr_buy_markout_by_name.csv (seller when trade is aggr_buy) and r4_passive_buyer_after_aggr_sell_markout_by_name.csv (buyer when aggr_sell); n≥20 per (passive_name,symbol,K). r4_phase1_passive_lp_summary.json: worst cells. Mark 14 passive seller on HYDRO after aggr buys: mean fwd20 −0.35 (CI crosses 0; t≈−0.93). Mark 14 passive *buyer* after aggr sells on extract/VEVs: mildly negative means at K=20 in several rows (see passive buyer CSV).
phase1_extras_against_prompt.mark67_day_stratify: r4_stratified_mark67_extract_by_day_session_spread_burst.csv — Mark67 extract aggr buy split by tape day × ts_bucket × spread_bucket × burst_ge4 (n≥10); 36 rows for multi-day stability checks.

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Mark 67 aggressive buys on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT show strongly positive mean forward mid at K=5,20 (n≈164, t≈13 and 5.8) with ~64–84% positive at short horizons; stable positive mean across days 1–3 (see r4_mark67_aggr_extract_fwd5_by_day.csv). Stratified table (tight BBO, isolated vs burst) confirms positive K=5 in each ts_bucket where n≥10 (see r4_stratified_markout_mark67_extract_tight_allK.csv). Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 at K=5 show negative mean fwd (−0.20, t≈−3.3, n=160) but Mark01→Mark22 on VEV_5300 reverses sign by day (day1 mean −0.70 vs day2 +0.18 in r4_cell_mean_fwd20_by_day_pair_symbol.csv) — not day-stable as a simple fade.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pair×symbol cell means + residuals)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Residual file supports second-order checks; r4_baseline_resid_pos_tail_n20.csv lists largest positive mean residuals by (buyer,seller,symbol) with n_cell≥20 (none dominate as uniform alpha without regime). r4_participant_net_flow_r4.csv: pair-count / gross-notional role sketch (e.g. Mark 14 high two-sided flow, Mark 67 buy-only in tape). Dominant Mark01→Mark22 cells on wings are near zero on average with day heterogeneity on VEV_5300 — no large uniform positive residual after simple cell baseline across all days.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 3) Sequence / graph (directed buyer→seller + 2-hop motifs vs extract fwd20)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Top directed pair Mark01→Mark22 matches sketch; two-hop Mark38→Mark14→Mark38 is frequent (n=160) with negative mean extract fwd20 (−0.58) while Mark55→Mark01→Mark22 (n=25) shows small positive mean (+0.16) — structural chains exist but need Phase-2 conditioning (burst, symbol) before trading.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 4) Burst structure (same day,timestamp ≥4 prints) vs random control
phase1_bullets.conclusion: 293 burst timestamps vs 879 control samples: mean VELVET forward-20-step mid change +0.33 vs +0.11 on control (r4_burst_vs_control_summary.json) — bursts associate with higher short-horizon extract drift on this slice.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 5) Adverse selection proxy (aggressive buy: passive seller forward mid)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Original aggr-buy→passive-seller table: worst mean fwd_mid_20 when Mark 14 passive seller on HYDRO (~−0.35, n=507). Extended passive-LP tables (n≥20 per name×symbol×K): same Mark14×HYDRO×K=20 mean −0.35 but bootstrap CI for mean crosses zero (t≈−0.9) — economically bad mean but not a sharp t-test; still a reasonable avoid-quote cell. Symmetric passive *buyer* after aggr sells: Mark14 on extract K=20 mean −0.24 (n=316, CI crosses 0) — mild adverse selection on both sides of Mark14 in some products. Mark22/Mark49 passive sellers on extract after aggr buys show large *positive* fwd (liquidity provision rewarded in those cells) — population heterogeneity, not uniform fade.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: After Mark 67 aggressive buy on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, short-horizon extract mid drifts up (K=5 mean ~+2.0, t≈13, n=164; day-split means ~1.75–2.2 on days 1–3).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Mid-only; not yet validated with worse fills or inventory.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Same-timestamp burst (≥4 prints) predicts higher extract forward 20-tick move than random non-burst times (+0.33 vs +0.11).
Mark 22 aggressive sell on VEV_5300 at K=5: negative mean forward mid on the same symbol (−0.20, t≈−3.3, n=160).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Mark01→Mark22 cell splits by day (5300 negative day1, positive day2) — use with regime split in Phase 2.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Frequent two-hop Mark38→Mark14→Mark38 coincides with negative extract fwd20 mean (−0.58, n=160).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect_size: −0.58 extract mid change 20 steps after second hop timestamp
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Consecutive-trade chain definition is coarse; refine with time windows.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Mark 14 as passive seller on HYDROGEL after aggressive buys shows most negative mean fwd_mid_20 in hydro slice (~−0.35, n=507).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Adverse-selection proxy only; needs quote-level sim.

negative_or_null_findings: Mark 38 aggressive flow on HYDROGEL_PACK shows no significant consistent directional fwd across K (|t|<1 in participant table).
Mark 55 aggressive sells on extract at K=100 are significantly negative (t≈−2.5) but short horizons weaker — mixed story, not a clean long signal.
Simple Mark01→Mark22 basket on VEV_5300 is not uniformly a fade across days (sign flip day 1 vs 2 in cell table).
Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5500 K=5 are negative but small magnitude (mean −0.026) — economically marginal vs tick size.
Mark 14 passive seller on HYDRO after aggressive buys: mean fwd20 negative (~−0.35) but bootstrap 95% CI for mean crosses zero (weak t); still a caution cell, not a sharp statistical lock.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 completion gate satisfied: all five Phase-1 bullets have CSV/JSON/txt outputs under manual_traders/R4/r4_counterparty_gated/analysis_outputs/. Proceed to Phase 2 (named-bot conditioning, microstructure, cross-lead–lag) only after strategy code consumes these edges; do not claim 200k/day until backtested.

title: round4_suggested_direction_phase1_crosswalk
analysis_description: Cross-walk: `round4work/suggested direction.txt` Phase 1 bullets vs `run_r4_phase1_analysis.py` + supplements. K ∈ {5,20,100} = number of *price-tape* row steps forward for the same (day, symbol) after the trade’s timestamp; `fwd_mid_K` = mid(t+K)−mid(t) on that series; `fwd_ext_K` = same for VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT. Concurrent BBO = price row at/just before trade ts (aggression: aggr_buy / aggr_sell / passive_mid). Spread quantile: per (day, symbol) quartile. Session: per-day *timestamp* quartile (`ts_bucket` 0–3), not wall-clock — R4 `timestamp` is tape index (0,100,…,999900). HYDROGEL forward for trade rows uses same-symbol series. **Median + bootstrap 95% CI for mean** are in `r4_participant_fwd_by_symbol.csv` (n≥20 for bootstrap CIs; see also `r4_participant_fwd_by_symbol_by_day.csv`).

suggested_direction_txt_bullet_status.1_participant_alpha_K_and_strata: Implemented: `r4_phase1_trade_events.csv`; pooled `r4_participant_fwd_by_symbol.csv`; by-day `r4_participant_fwd_by_symbol_by_day.csv`; **stratified** `r4_stratified_markout_by_session_spread_burst.csv` + `run_r4_phase1_stratify_markouts.py` (session ts_bucket × spread quartile × burst_ge4, n≥10; mean/median/t/boot CI).
suggested_direction_txt_bullet_status.1_cross_asset_extract: Implemented: `fwd_ext_{K}` columns in trade-level data used in two-hop and burst summaries; top-line participant table is per-trade symbol; Mark67 extract day stability in `r4_mark67_aggr_extract_fwd5_by_day.csv`.
suggested_direction_txt_bullet_status.1_median_bootstrap: Automated: `r4_participant_fwd_by_symbol.csv` includes median_fwd, boot_ci95_mean_lo/hi (2000 resamples, min n=20 for CI). t_stat still requires n≥30 in script.
suggested_direction_txt_bullet_status.1_hour_real_clock: Not used; tape time deciles added separately: `run_r4_phase1_m67_tape_time_buckets.py` → `r4_m67_extract_fwd5_by_tape_decile_pooled.csv` (Mark67 extract aggr buy fwd5 by `timestamp//100000` 0..9) — M67 long signal stays >0 in every decile on days 1–3.
suggested_direction_txt_bullet_status.2_bot_baseline_residuals: Cell means `r4_cell_mean_fwd20_by_day_pair_symbol.csv`, global baseline + residual `r4_trade_level_residual_fwd20.csv` (not GBM).
suggested_direction_txt_bullet_status.3_graph_motifs: Edges `r4_directed_edges_buyer_seller.csv` (count, sum|price|, gross|p*q|), `r4_edge_pairs_with_reciprocity.csv`, `r4_top20_directed_edge_pairs.csv`, `r4_node_degree_in_out_top30.csv`; two-hop `r4_twohop_motif_fwd_extract20.csv` (not full triad enumeration).
suggested_direction_txt_bullet_status.4_bursts: `r4_burst_event_rows.csv`, `r4_burst_vs_control_summary.json` (n≥4 extract fwd); `r4_burst_orchestrator_by_timestamp.csv` (mode buyer/seller for each (day,ts) with 2+ prints).

used_to_decide: Phase 1 completion gate in `round4work/suggested direction.txt` remains satisfied: every bullet has quantitative output; the crosswalk names explicit gaps (bootstrap/medians, true clock hour) and points to the tape-time decile supplement for session-like stratification. Phases 2–3 were already complete in this file; `trader_v11` remains the execution champion vs earlier joint-only sizing experiments.

title: round4_phase2_complete
analysis_description: Phase 2 scripts: run_r4_phase2_analysis.py (burst-conditioned fwd20, signed-flow lead–lag vs extract fwd20, microprice file, spread-tight regime table, M01→M22 burst residuals) + run_r4_phase2_build_signals.py (Mark67 keys for live tick). trader_v1 encodes spread-tight + optional Mark67 lift; backtest R4 days 1–3.

phase2_bullets.conclusion: Burst-conditioned table shows Mark67→Mark22 extract prints have high mean fwd20 when burst_type=no_burst (n=73); Mark67 aggr_buy split burst vs no_burst is inconclusive (only n=2 in burst bucket). Mark01→Mark22 burst rows on VEV_5200 show positive mean fwd20 (n=11) while same pair on VEV_5300 negative (n=131) — refines Phase-1 pair story: basket burst structure matters by strike.
Extract BBO spread<=2 (tight=1): mean fwd_mid_20 ≈ +1.16 (n=1217) vs tight=0 mean ≈ −0.056 (n=28723) — strong regime split orthogonal to counterparty IDs; trader_v1 uses this as primary gate.
Same-tick signed extract flow vs subsequent extract fwd20 corr ≈ +0.13 at lag 0, weaker at 1–5 — mild short-horizon momentum co-movement, not a standalone large edge.
Same spread-tight table: signal survives only in tight book regime for forward-mid stats (aligns with R3 Sonic intuition extended to R4 extract tape).
Not run in this pass (no IV column in tape); deferred to Phase 3 or dedicated BS-from-mids pass.
Phase-1 file reused: Mark14 passive seller on hydro after aggr buys remains worst cell — trader_v1 does not yet fade hydro vs Mark14 (future rule).
Not implemented in trader_v1 (no inventory model); Phase-2 evidence only.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Extract tight BBO (spread <= 2) associates with much higher mean 20-tick forward mid (+1.16 vs -0.06); encoded as primary regime in trader_v1.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Confirms Phase-1 burst-vs-control (extract drifts) through a simpler always-on microstructure gate not requiring counterparty IDs.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Mark01→Mark22 burst on VEV_5200: positive mean fwd20 in burst-conditioned table (small n=11) vs negative on VEV_5300 in same burst — strike-specific basket follow-through.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Refines Phase-1 Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300 day-heterogeneity: conditioning on burst_type separates 5200 vs 5300 behavior.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Signed aggressive extract flow same-tick correlates positively (≈0.13) with extract fwd20 — weak momentum scaler potential.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Orthogonal to Mark67 participant alpha; adds order-flow dimension.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Mark67 extract aggressive buys remain top Phase-1 signal; Phase-2 burst split is data-starved — keep Mark67 tick keys as optional lift only (trader_v1).
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Partially refines Mark67 story: burst conditioning does not strengthen (n=2 bursts); unconditioned Phase-1 effect remains the evidence base.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Residual re-centering inside Mark01→Mark22 bursts vs global pair mean (r4_phase2_residual_fwd20_within_M01_M22_burst.csv) for second-order / fade research.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Builds on Phase-1 residual file with explicit burst slice.

phase1_interaction_summary: Phase-1 Mark67 extract effect is left intact as optional signal; Phase-2 spread-tight regime is a stronger always-on tape split. Phase-1 Mark01→Mark22 5300 ambiguity is partially explained by separating burst vs non-burst and by strike (5200 vs 5300).
used_to_decide: Shipped trader_v1 (spread regime + Mark67 JSON keys). Backtest: worse total 26,194 vs v0 0 (active MM). Phase 3 next: Sonic joint VEV5200+5300 gate on R4 + counterparty interactions per ping_followup_phases.md.

title: round4_phase3_complete
analysis_description: Phase 3: R4 Sonic joint gate (inner-join 5200/5300/extract on timestamp, tight = both BBO spreads <=2, K=20 fwd on aligned panel per analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py). inclineGod: spread–spread and spread vs extract mid change correlations (full tape vs joint_tight subset). Counterparty rows merged to gate for triplet (pair,symbol,joint_tight) fwd20 table. trader_v2 implements joint gate + fallback extract-tight.

sonic_gate_vs_unconditional.pooled_K20: mean extract fwd20 tight +0.32 (n=7706) vs loose -0.12 (n=22274) — same sign all three days (r4_phase3_sonic_gate_summary.json).
sonic_gate_vs_unconditional.phase2_extract_only_tight: Phase2 used extract BBO<=2 alone: mean fwd20 +1.16 vs -0.06 — larger magnitude because conditioning is on extract microstructure directly, not ATM voucher legs.
sonic_gate_vs_unconditional.does_gate_clean_counterparty: Mixed: Mark67→Mark22 extract mean is higher when joint_tight (n=15 mean 2.83 vs n=60 mean 1.83 in triplet table) — gate helps that interaction. Mark01→Mark22 on VEV_5300 only occurs under joint_tight (all 132 prints) with negative mean fwd20 (-0.12) — falsifies simple long 5300 after basket when surface is tight; Phase2 strike split (5200 +0.86 vs 5300 -0.12 in bursts) holds under gate for 5300.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase3_ranked.edge: Sonic joint gate on R4: pooled extract 20-tick forward mid +0.32 vs -0.12 when loose — stable sign days 1–3.
Mark67→Mark22 on extract: higher mean fwd20 when joint_tight vs not (triplet CSV; small n tight=15).
Spread–spread: s5200 vs s5300 corr ~0.31 on full R4 panel, ~0.17 when restricted to joint_tight rows — co-movement weakens in tight box (inclineGod lens).
Mark14→Mark38 VEV_4000 under joint_tight: mean fwd20 ~+1.1 (n=55) — three-way (pair, gate, symbol) candidate.
Mark67 aggressive extract buy: slightly higher mean fwd20 when gate tight (n=27 vs n=137 loose) — aligns Phase1 Mark67 with Sonic gate.

sim_note: trader_v2 (joint gate sizing) backtest worse 21,097 < trader_v1 26,194 — joint gate alone as implemented does not improve PnL vs extract-only tight regime; keep gate for signal research, refine execution.
used_to_decide: Phase 3 gate analysis persisted; trader_v2 shipped as Sonic-gated experiment underperforming v1 on this simple MM — next iteration should combine joint gate with selective fades (e.g. avoid aggressive 5300 basket supply) rather than blanket size-up.

title: round4_post_phase3_execution_experiments
analysis_description: After Phase 3: (1) tape merge of joint gate to trades shows VEV_5300 notional is ~99% when joint_tight vs loose (r4_trade_notional_by_symbol_gate.csv) — M01→M22 ladder concentrates in Sonic-tight clock; skipping 5300 MM should matter if we had inventory. (2) trader_v3 widened/shrank 5300 under joint tight vs v2 — PnL unchanged (21097 worse) because quotes clamp to BBO so half-spread edits often ineffective. (3) trader_v4 skips 5300 entirely under joint tight — still 21097 worse / 19230 all: PnL dominated by extract and hydro same as v2.
used_to_decide: Champion for this MM family remains trader_v1 (26,194 worse). Next: inventory-aware 5300 or take-side logic keyed to market_trades counterparty (requires richer sim than quote-only half-width nudges).

analysis_description: Tape-scheduled counterparty fade (no runtime market_trades in trader.run): built signals_m01_m22_5300_sonic_tight.json (132 ticks) where historical trade is Mark01→Mark22 on VEV_5300 AND Sonic joint gate holds on tape at same (day,timestamp). trader_v5 = v1 + skip VEV_5300 bid only on those ticks when live joint gate is on.
used_to_decide: Backtest PnL ties trader_v1 exactly (26,194 worse / 24,301 all): selective bid removal on 132 clock ticks does not change aggregate fills vs naive MM — either bids were not marginal at those instants or inventory path nets out. Validates rule as safe overlay; next step is aggressive take/fade using same key set or own_trades feedback.

analysis_description: Backtester hook: test_runner.__initialize_trade_state now sets state.market_trades to a shallow copy of tape trades at the current timestamp (symbol -> list[Trade] with buyer/seller) before Trader.run. OrderMatchMaker still rebuilds from BacktestData for matching; strategies can read counterparty IDs without precomputed JSON. trader_v6 mirrors v5 logic using live market_trades instead of signals_m01_m22_5300_sonic_tight.json; PnL still 26,194/24301 (same as v1/v5).
used_to_decide: Enables future Round-4 agents to branch on buyer/seller per tick without offline key files; v1 sanity unchanged after patch.

analysis_description: Tape script run_r4_basket_wing_stats.py: among 132 joint-tight timestamps with a Mark01→Mark22 print on VEV_5300, 108 also have Mark01→Mark22 on each of VEV_5300–6500 in the same tick (frac 0.818). trader_v7 extends v6 by skipping bids on that full ladder when joint tight + full-ladder M01→M22 co-print holds.
used_to_decide: Backtest worse 26,194 and all 24,301 match v1/v5/v6 exactly (same trade counts and notionals). The coordinated-ladder bid suppression never moves the fill path versus single-5300 fade: either those ticks were not marginal for our bids, or PnL is dominated by extract/hydro as in prior iterations. Hypothesis falsified for this MM implementation; champion remains v1 unless we add inventory-aware or aggressive take logic keyed to counterparty.

analysis_description: trader_v8 = v6 + integer inventory skew on voucher quotes: shift bid and ask by min(3, floor(|pos|/50)) ticks against the sign of position (long lowers both sides to discourage more buys). Tape can build large VEV_4000 inventory (e.g. cum qty 876 in tradeHistory on v8 worse log) so the rule is active in principle.
used_to_decide: Worse 26,194 / all 24,301 and parsed trade aggregates are identical to v6/v7 (4484/4486 trades, same top-symbol notionals). So under BBO clamping and this step size, skew collapses to the same effective prices as v6 for the realized fill path. trader_v9 (see iteration 9) increases skew step/max to break the collapse.

analysis_description: trader_v9 = v6 + stronger inventory skew (INV_SKEW_STEP=25, INV_SKEW_MAX=8 ticks). Same counterparty fade and Mark67 keys as v6/v8.
used_to_decide: First measurable deviation from the v1/v6 plateau: worse total 26,196 (+2 vs 26,194), all 24,304 (+3 vs 24,301). Trade count unchanged (4484/4486); gross_notional slightly lower; VEV_4000 notional 1,092,534 vs 1,092,611 — skew moved some voucher fills. Day 1 worse PnL +2 (15,434 vs 15,432). Treat as small win; next sweep could tune step/max or apply skew to extract when |pos_u| is large, or combine with Mark67 aggressive buy (take) per Phase 1 edge.

analysis_description: trader_v10 extends v9 (voucher inventory skew step 25 max 8; M01→M22 5300 fade; Mark67 JSON half-width lift) with (a) extract bid/ask shifted by min(3, |pos_u|//40) ticks vs inventory sign, (b) extra aggressive extract buy Order(price=best_ask, qty=22) when extract BBO tight AND day:timestamp in signals_mark67_aggr_extract_buy.json and room under 200 limit.
used_to_decide: Large PnL lift vs v9 on Round 4 days 1–3: worse 30,256 (+4,060 vs 26,196), all 30,421 (+6,117 vs 24,304). Extract trade count rises (1529 vs 1381 worse; 1573 vs 1381 all) with higher extract notional and gross tape notional; hydro/VEV counts unchanged vs v9. Day splits worse: day1 16,360 (+926), day2 7,878 (+6,660), day3 6,019 (−3,525 vs v9) — day2 dominates the gain. Validates combining Phase-1 Mark67 keyed aggression with existing MM under worse fills; next: tune M67 take size/cap, optional joint_tight gate on the take, or reduce day3 drawdown.

analysis_description: Tape overlap: run_r4_m67_joint_overlap.py joins signals_mark67_aggr_extract_buy.json keys to price rows; 27/164 keys (16.5%) have VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 BBO spreads both ≤2 at that timestamp (by day: 5/58 day1, 7/61 day2, 15/45 day3). trader_v11 = v10 but aggressive Mark67 take only when extract tight AND joint tight.
used_to_decide: Worse total 30,932 (+676 vs v10 30,256); all 33,466 (+3,045 vs v10 30,421). Fewer trades (4511 vs 4632 worse) and lower extract notional than v10; gross_notional 86.43M vs 92.30M. Day splits worse: day1 16,361 (+1 vs v10), day2 5,255 (−2,623 vs v10), day3 9,316 (+3,297 vs v10) — gating the take mostly removes the v10 day-2 bump while improving day 3. New champion for worse and all on this branch; keep v10 as ungated-take reference.

analysis_description: trader_v12 = v11-style stack but Mark67 aggressive take on every extract-tight key tick: buy at ask with qty 8 when Sonic joint is loose, qty 22 when joint tight (v11 only fires take under joint).
used_to_decide: Worse 30,842 (−90 vs v11 30,932); all 33,860 (+394 vs v11 33,466). More extract activity than v11 (1542 vs 1408 extract trades worse; gross_notional 91.6M vs 86.4M) but worse-match PnL regresses — the extra loose-book takes do not pay under --match-trades worse on this tape. Champion remains v11 for worse; v12 only wins on all mode.

analysis_description: trader_v13 = trader_v11 with joint-gated Mark67 take quantity raised from 22 to 35 at best ask (same gates and rest of logic as v11).
used_to_decide: Worse 30,932 and all 34,188: identical worse PnL and trade summary to v11 (4511 trades, same extract row); all mode +722 vs v11 likely from marginal fill-path tie-breaks without changing worse economics. Conclude 22→35 does not move realized worse outcomes here; keep v11 take size.

analysis_description: trader_v14 = v11 + inventory guard on the joint-gated Mark67 take only: require extract long position ≤85 before placing the aggressive buy at ask (qty 22). Intended to skip takes when already heavily long.
used_to_decide: Worse 30,932 / all 33,466: numerically identical to v11 on this tape (4511 worse trades, same extract stats). So no joint+M67 take tick in the backtest had pu>85 at order time; the guard is inactive here. Keep v11 as champion; v14 documents the null experiment.

analysis_description: trader_v15 = v11 with joint-gated Mark67 take qty reduced 22→16 at best ask.
used_to_decide: Worse still 30,932 (same 4511-trade path as v11); all 33,452 (−14 vs v11) with 4532 trades and slightly lower extract notional—smaller takes add noise in all mode without helping worse. Prefer v11 take size 22.

analysis_description: trader_v16 = v14 logic with stricter long cap M67_TAKE_MAX_LONG=50 before allowing the joint Mark67 take.
